com.mentor.chs.plugin

Interface IXBaseApplicationContext

  • All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXApplicationContext, IXSymbolContext

    @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXBaseApplicationContext
    This object allows any IXPlugin to have access to a number of objects that are common to the Design and Symbol applications.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.String getApplicationRoot()
      Returns the application root (home) directory.
      IXOutputWindow getOutputWindow()
      Returns an IXOutputWindow object that allows the plugins, like Custom Actions, to display information to the output window.
      java.awt.Frame getParentFrame()
      Returns the parent frame to be used when creating JDialog components.
      boolean invokeAction(java.lang.String url, java.awt.event.ActionEvent e)
      This method can be used to invoke another plugin action.
      void show(IXObject obj, boolean zoomSelected, boolean addToSelection)
      This method can be used to select an object in the diagram and\or to do zoom select from plugins

    • Method Detail

      • getOutputWindow

        IXOutputWindow getOutputWindow()

        Returns an IXOutputWindow object that allows the plugins, like Custom Actions, to display information to the output window. For applications like, Capital Design, Capital Integrator, Capital Logic and Capital HarnessXC, the output window is the window at the bottom of the main application window.

        Returns:
        the output window.

      • getParentFrame

        java.awt.Frame getParentFrame()
        Returns the parent frame to be used when creating JDialog components. Here is the eIXBxample of using this parent frame in an IXAction plugin: 
            boolean execute(IXApplicationContext applicationContext) {
                JDialog dialog = new JDialog(applicationContext.getParentFrame(), true);
      dialog.setTitle(getName());
      //...
      // create your dialog contents here...
      //...
      dialog.setVisible(true);
      return true;
       }
        Returns:
        the parent frame. This method could return null in situations were there is no GUI available.

      • getApplicationRoot

        java.lang.String getApplicationRoot()
        Returns the application root (home) directory. You should use this method in preference to using System.getenv() as the environment variable used may be different in other types of applications and the method will return the correct value in all situations.
        Returns:
        the application root (home) directory.

      • show

        void show(IXObject obj,
          boolean zoomSelected,
          boolean addToSelection)
        This method can be used to select an object in the diagram and\or to do zoom select from plugins
        Parameters:
        obj - Object to be selected. Object can be a diagram object or connectivity object or it can be a diagram. If it is a diagram then this API becomes a mechanism to open a diagram from the plugin. If it is a connectivity, this API tries to open all diagrams and select the objects in those diagrams. If it is Diagram then it API just opens or switches to this diagram.

        If the diagram is part of another design, the design will be opened in the current application session and remain opened after plug-in has finished its execution unless explicitly closed before then. Where possible the design will be opened for editing, otherwise, it will be opened in read-only mode.

        In the future, if the current application does not support the type of design that needs to be opened, this method may open the appropriate application if installed and licenses are available.

        zoomSelected - True to zoom to selected object in the diagram.
        addToSelection - False to to clear previous selection and add the objects to the selection. If True it just adds to the selection.

      • invokeAction

        boolean invokeAction(java.lang.String url,
                     java.awt.event.ActionEvent e)
        This method can be used to invoke another plugin action. Action will be invoked only if no other action is active. This is generally useful in design inspector to launch actions from the design inspector
        Parameters:
        url - URL of custom action to be executed. URL should be of specific format eg:- plugin:Java//com.example.plugin.action.ChangeAttributeAction URL form of a custom action can be obtained from File->Plugins->Details Dialog
        e - ActionEvent generated while invoking action.
        Returns:
        true if action is managed to be executed. false if action with given url is not found or if action couldn't be run due to other reasons like another action running.
